description_long: This object is a rectangular, box-shaped electronic device with a sturdy metal housing, featuring a matte light gray upper section and a black lower section. One end of the device has a prominent cylindrical lens or sensor, suggesting it may be a specialized camera, sensor, or projector. The opposite end displays a control panel with several buttons, switches, indicator lights, and a power socket, as well as a thick, coiled power cable extending outward. Ventilation slots are visible on the top surface, indicating the need for internal cooling. The overall design is industrial and robust, suitable for use in technical or utility environments such as surveillance, monitoring, or laboratory settings. Branding and model information are printed on the side and front, with the name 'Leitz' and 'Pradovit' visible, hinting at a possible connection to optical or projection equipment.
description: A robust, rectangular electronic device with a metal housing, cylindrical lens, control panel, and power cable, likely used for technical or optical applications.
description_view_0: Side view showing the rectangular body, cylindrical lens, and attached power cable.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the control panel, power socket, switches, and cable connection.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the cable looped and the lens partially visible.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the cylindrical lens and branding, with the cable trailing behind.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
